fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 70 is inverted: `if not clamp` applies clamping when clamp is False and skips it when clamp is True, which is the opposite of the intended behavior.

constraints.intent: When clamp is True (default), the branch that performs clamping should be entered — but with `if not clamp`, it is skipped.
The then-branch (clamping logic) should only execute when clamp is False, confirming the inverted condition.

spec.expr: not clamp and min(y0, y1) <= y__2 <= max(y0, y1)

constraints.intent: y__2 (the clamped value) should only be computed when clamp is False, and must lie within [min(y0,y1), max(y0,y1)].
When clamp is True, the returned value must be within [min(y0,y1), max(y0,y1)].
